AI · Quick Guide

Getting Started with Gemini CLI

← Back to Course Package

1. Key Concepts

  • Command Line Interface (CLI): A text-based environment that allows users to interact with Gemini models directly from their terminal without a web browser.
  • Model Context Protocol (MCP): An open standard that enables the AI to securely connect to external data sources like databases and local files.
  • Vibe Coding: A high-level development approach where you provide descriptions or mockups while the AI handles the technical implementation.
  • YOLO Mode: A specialized setting that grants the CLI permission to execute commands and write files automatically without manual confirmation.
  • Multimodal Input: The capability of the Gemini CLI to process and understand different data formats, including text and images, simultaneously.

2. Syntax / API Reference

Use these commands to manage your environment and model interactions:

  • Global Installation: npm install -g @google/generative-ai-cli
  • Set API Key: export GEMINI_API_KEY='your_key_here'
  • Launch CLI: gemini
  • Initialize Project Context: /init (Creates a gemini.md file for project-specific instructions)
  • Change Model: /model gemini-1.5-pro
  • Clear Terminal: /clear
  • Exit CLI: /exit or Ctrl+C

3. Step-by-Step Workflow

  1. Environment Setup: Ensure Node.js and npm are installed on your machine; Mac users may also need Xcode command line tools.
  2. Installation: Run the npm global install command to add the Gemini CLI to your system path.
  3. Authentication: Add your GEMINI_API_KEY to your shell profile (e.g., .zshrc or .bashrc) to automate logins.
  4. Project Initialization: Navigate to your project folder and run /init to generate a context file that defines your project goals.
  5. Interaction: Type natural language prompts to generate code, analyze files, or perform web searches directly in the terminal.
  6. Extension Integration: Install external tools like Google Workspace extensions to connect your AI to your documents and emails.

4. Gotcha Alerts

  • ⚠️ Missing Dependencies: The CLI will fail if Node.js is not installed first, as npm is bundled with the Node.js runtime.
  • ⚠️ Naming Sensitivity: The environment variable must be named exactly GEMINI_API_KEY or the CLI will prompt for manual login every time.
  • ⚠️ Auth Conflicts: You cannot run an API Key session and a Google Account login session simultaneously in the same terminal instance.
  • ⚠️ Extension Management: You must exit the Gemini CLI environment back to the standard shell to install new system-level extensions.

5. Key Takeaways

  • Navigate the Gemini ecosystem using terminal commands instead of a graphical user interface.
  • Automate file creation and system tasks using YOLO mode for faster development cycles.
  • Leverage project-specific context by utilizing gemini.md files to keep the AI informed of your local environment.
  • Connect the AI to real-world data and tools using the Model Context Protocol and external extensions.
